LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID LEVY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 5TH-9TH CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. REGARDING THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES WITH REASONABLE PARTICULARITY AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612, 619.) AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HOW AND WHY A FIDUCIARY DUTY IS OWED BY THIS DEFENDANT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT LEVY, DAVID

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN MARKETING COMPLEX, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is to be heard at 9:00 am in Dept. 301 on line 5 of the discovery calendar. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Plaintiff American Marketing Complex, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Answer Or In The Alternative To Strike Affirmative Defenses, As Discovery Sanctions For For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS D. MAURIELLO, TEL. NO. (949) 542-3555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JAMES EDWARD MCDANIEL OF STATEWIDE COUNSEL, TEL. NO. (805) 468-5761 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff American Marketing Complex, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Answer Or In The Alternative To Strike Affirmative Defenses, As Discovery Sanctions and For Monetary Sanctions IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT CARDIAC NETWORK INCORPORATED'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE STRUCK. SANCTION AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,250.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. David Mcdonald, Judge Pro-tem, Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark O. Van Wagoner, Esq., of Law Offices of Mark O. Van Wagoner, Tel. 801.487.6882, appearing for Defendant and moving party via Court Call.
Thomas D. Mauriello, Esq., of Mauriello Law Firm, APC, Tel. 949.542.3555, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party via Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STANDARD REGISTER & TRANSFER CO., INC.'S (Re-noticed) Motion To Quash Service Of Summoms For Lack Of Jurisdiction is Granted. Plaintiff has not met its burden of showing by a preponderance of the evidence that defendant Standard Register has sufficient contacts with California to warrant the exercise of either general or specific jurisdiction. Mr. Van Wagoner to submit a proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Off Calendar Sep-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT DAVID LEVY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 5TH-9TH CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. REGARDING THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES WITH REASONABLE PARTICULARITY AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612, 619.) AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HOW AND WHY A FIDUCIARY DUTY IS OWED BY THIS DEFENDANT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
